svg.text: The Financial Market as a Multi-Level Competitive System
50,000+ institutional players · 500M+ retail participants · 30 central banks · all playing simultaneously — at different timescales, objectives, and information sets
LEVEL I — SOVEREIGN & MACRO LAYER · Mean-Field Control (MFC): Social Planner Objective
Timescale: months–years · Objective: price stability / economic mandate / capital allocation · Instruments: interest rates, QE, regulation, tariffs
Basel III · SIFI rules · capital floors
Sets rules for ALL levels below
↓ Policy: rates · capital controls · regulatory limits on leverage · reserve requirements
LEVEL II — INSTITUTIONAL PLAYER LAYER · Mean-Field Game (MFG): Nash Equilibrium
Timescale: days–months · Objective: risk-adjusted return · Interact: via price impact, information asymmetry, prime brokerage
PMs compete for capital allocation · risk budgets
But also: front-running risk? Information asymmetry!
Price-agnostic flow amplifies volatility (MFG shows why)
Long-horizon mandate collides with mark-to-market losses
MFG mixed: MFC internally + Nash vs. peers
↓ Capital allocation · risk budget · return targets ↑ P&L contribution · position reports
LEVEL III — MICRO AGENT LAYER · Individual Optimization + Intra-Firm Competition
Timescale: milliseconds–weeks · Objective: personal/desk P&L · Interact: order flow, signal sharing, risk limit negotiation
Desks compete for capital: ← "I need more VaR budget" →
Risk mgmt cooperates: sets firm-wide limits, prevents blow-up
This intra-firm tension = MFG within each institution (nested game)
500 portfolio managers at Millennium: each is a sovereign within their pod
Aggregating ξ^{j,u}: pod-level MFG → firm-level MFG → market-level MFG
Robinhood / moomoo / Tiger Brokers → zero commission
Same LLMs for investment advice → AI-homogenised flow
Reddit / Twitter coordination → behavioral ν_η spikes
GameStop (Jan 2021): ν_η rose 46×, σ_τ barely moved
In our model: Level III agents with Lévy jump noise
Their aggregate flow enters Level II via mean-field μ^(3)_{retail}
As LLMs homogenise retail → behavioral term increasingly model-able
HFT Ecosystem (~50% of US equity volume)
Market-makers: Virtu, Citadel Sec., Jane Street — quote competition
Latency arms race: co-location at NYSE · nanosecond advantages
Bid-ask spread = Nash equilibrium of market-maker game
Our model: Level III MFC — social welfare = minimize transaction cost
Emergent: bid-ask spreads & market depth from Level III equilibrium
Input to Level II: determines execution cost and slippage for all above
HFT Level III ← most frequent MFG update: every 15min
MARKET PRICE = EMERGENT NASH EQUILIBRIUM · P(t) = ∫∫∫ α*(x,ξ,m) · m^(3)(dx) · m^(2)(dξ) · ν^(1)(dΓ)
The price you observe on your screen is the real-time summary statistic of this entire three-level game — updated every millisecond. No single player controls it. Every player influences it.
MFG/MFC institution · model type shown in each box
